notes: FiveThirtyEight wrote on August 5, 2020 that Biden was up by roughly 8 points in the national polling average.
Axios summarized early-October national polling with Biden at roughly 51 and Trump at roughly 41.
Wikipedia's archived FiveThirtyEight tracker summary lists Biden 51.8 and Trump 43.4 through November 2, 2020.
The race still favored Fetterman comfortably in late summer before the October tightening.
FiveThirtyEight wrote on October 7, 2022 that Fetterman had fallen from an 11-point lead on September 13 to roughly a 7-point lead.
Wikipedia's archived FiveThirtyEight tracker summary lists Oz 47.4 and Fetterman 46.9 through November 7, 2022.
The Guardian summarized FiveThirtyEight's national average on August 9, 2024 as Harris ahead by about 2.1 points.
Fox 5 DC summarized FiveThirtyEight's national average on October 3, 2024 as Harris ahead by about 2.8 points.
A contemporaneous aggregate roundup reported the national polling average at Harris 47.9 and Trump 46.7 on November 4, 2024.
